insights.title: Renamed Class to Follow Convention
insights.details: The refactored class name from "UserManager" to "UsernameManager" follows the conventional camelCase naming convention for Java classes.

insights.title: Simplified Variable Naming
insights.details: Variable names have been simplified from "n" to "username", improving readability and consistency with other variable names in the codebase.

insights.title: Improved Code Readability through Consistent Accessor Names
insights.details: The refactored class uses consistent accessor (getter) name conventions, following Java's standard naming convention for getter methods (e.g., "getUsername" instead of "getN").
